note: Do not instantiate directly; use itasca.structure module functions.

methods.name: area
methods.description: Get surface area of the Shell element.

methods.name: beta
methods.description: Get Shell system beta angle. This is defined as the angle between the element local system x-axis and the material system x-axis. In radians measured counter-clockwise from the element local x-axis.

methods.name: component_id
methods.description: Get the Shell component id.

methods.name: connectivity
methods.description: Get the index of the node with component-ID id in the element. A ValueError exception is raised if no node in that element matches.

methods.name: delete
methods.description: Delete this structural element.

methods.name: density
methods.description: Get the density of this structural element.

methods.name: depth_factor
methods.description: Get the depth factor at which stresses have been recovered. The depth equals Ft/2 where t is the Shell element thickness. F values equal to +1.0 or -1.0 correspond to the outer/inner Shell surface. The outer surface is defined by the positive z-direction of the local Shell element system. F values of 0.0 corresponds to the Shell mid-surface. The depth factor can be modified with the \(structure Shell recover stress\) command.

methods.name: element_type
methods.description: Get the name of the finite-element type used by this Shell element. The possible types for Shell elements are ‘cst’, ‘csth’, ‘dkt’, ‘dkt-cst’, and ‘dkt-csth’.

methods.name: extra
methods.description: Get the Shell extra data in the given slot.

methods.name: force_nodal
methods.description: Get the generalized nodal force acting on the Shell at each node. The translational and rotational components of node 1 are given first.

methods.name: group
methods.description: Get the Shell group name in a given slot.

methods.name: group_remove
methods.signature: shell.group_remove(group_name: str or int[, slot: str or int]) -> bool
methods.description: Remove from the given group from all group slots of the Shell. One argument of type string, giving the group name, is required. The return value is a bool which is True if the group was removed from any slot, otherwise False.

methods.name: groups
methods.description: Get a dictionary describing which groups this Shell is part of. The keys of the dictionary are the slot names and the values are the group names.

methods.name: id
methods.description: Get the Shell structural element id.

methods.name: in_group
methods.signature: shell.in_group(group_name: str or int[, slot: str or int]) -> bool
methods.description: Test if the Shell is part of a given group. If the optional argument slot is given, only that slot is searched. Otherwise, all group slots are searched.

methods.name: local_system
methods.description: Get local coordinate system of this element as a 3x3 tensor.

methods.name: mark
methods.description: Get the mark flag on the structural element. The structural element logic may modify this flag during command processing and cycling.

methods.name: nodes
methods.signature: shell.nodes() -> tuple of Structural element node objects
methods.description: Get a tuple containing the structural element nodes attached to this elemment.

returns.type: tuple of Structural element node objects

methods.name: poisson
methods.description: Get the Poisson’s ratio of the element.

methods.name: pos
methods.description: Get the position vector of the element, defined as the element centroid.

methods.name: pressure
methods.description: Get the uniform pressure acting on the Shell. A positive value acts in the z-direction of the element local system.

methods.name: prop_anis
methods.signature: shell.prop_anis() -> (membrane: tuple of float, bending: tuple of float)
methods.description: Get the anisotropic material properties. Material-stiffness coefficients c’11, c’12, c’13, c’22, c’23, c’33 are returned for both the membrane and bending stiffnesses in that order. If the element does not have anisotropic properties, zeros are returned.

returns.type: (membrane: tuple of float, bending: tuple of float)

methods.name: prop_ortho
methods.signature: shell.prop_ortho() -> (membrane: tuple of float, bending: tuple of float)
methods.description: Get the orthotropic material properties. Material-stiffness coefficients c’11, c’12, c’13, c’22, c’23, c’33 are returned for both the membrane and bending stiffnesses in that order. If the element does not have orthotropic properties, zeros are returned.

returns.type: (membrane: tuple of float, bending: tuple of float)

methods.name: prop_type
methods.description: Get the material property type. Possible return values are ‘isotropic’, ‘orthotripc’, and ‘anisotropic’.

methods.name: resultant
methods.signature: shell.resultant(location: int) -> length 8 tuple of float
methods.description: Get stress resultants last calculated for the element at the index \(location\). A \(location\) value of 0 gives the resultant at the element centroid; \(location\) values 1, 2 and 3 give the resultant at the nodes. The components in the return value are: Mx, My, Mxy, Nx, Ny, Nxy, Qx and Qy. Note that stress resultants must be calculated with the \(structure Shell recover\) command. See also the \(resultant_valid\) method.

methods.name: resultant_valid
methods.description: Get stress resultant validity flag. True indicates that the stress resultant values are valid. False indicates that resultants have not been calculated or that there has been a change since they were last calculated that would invalidate the results.

methods.name: set_density
methods.description: Set the density of this structural element.

methods.name: set_extra
methods.description: Set the Shell extra data in the given slot.

methods.name: set_group
methods.signature: shell.set_group(group_name: str or int[, slot: str or int]) -> None
methods.description: Set the Shell group name in a given slot.

methods.name: set_mark
methods.description: Set the mark flag on the structural element. The structural element logic may modify this flag during command processing and cycling.

methods.name: set_pressure
methods.description: Set the uniform pressure acting on the Shell. A positive value acts in the z-direction of the element local system.

methods.name: set_thermal_expansion
methods.description: Set the thermal-expansion coefficient for the structural element.

methods.name: set_thickness
methods.description: Set the element thickness.

methods.name: stress
methods.description: Get the Shell stresses at the current depth factor at the index \(location\). A \(location\) value of 0 gives the stress at the element centroid; \(location\) values 1, 2 and 3 give the stress at the nodes. Stresses are expressed in the global system. Note that compressive stresses are negative.

methods.name: stress_max
methods.description: Get the maximum stress of the element. This is the maximum principal stress of the fiber stress at depth +1 or -1, which is the combination of membrane and bending resultants.

methods.name: stress_prin
methods.description: Get the resolved principal stress at the current depth factor at the index \(location\). A \(location\) value of 0 gives the stress at the element centroid; \(location\) values 1, 2 and 3 give the stress at the nodes. Returns a vector encoded as (minimum, intermediate, maximum). Note that compressive stresses are negative in FLAC3D.

methods.name: stress_valid
methods.description: Get the stress validity flag for the element. True indicates that the stresses last calculated at the current depth factor are valid. Galse indicates that stresses have not been calculated, or that there has been a change that invalidates the last stresses calculated.

methods.name: thermal_expansion
methods.description: Get the thermal-expansion coefficient for the structural element.

methods.name: thickness
methods.description: Get the element thickness.

methods.name: valid
methods.description: Returns True if this Shell is live.

methods.name: volume
methods.description: Get the element volume, or surface area times thickness.

methods.name: young
methods.description: Get the Youngs modulus of the element. Modifying the value during cycling may be a danger.
